body:
## Review Summary

The error context added here will be useful for debugging snap sync validation failures. The change from `TrieError::Verify` to `TrieError::InconsistentTree(NodeCountMismatch)` is the right call — it aligns with how other trie structural errors are reported.

Main concern: `compute_hash().finalize()` is called on every node during iteration (see inline at line 568) even though it's only needed if validation fails. On Mainnet state tries with hundreds of millions of nodes, this adds non-trivial overhead to an already expensive validation path.

comments.body: nit: `path` is owned and not used after this line in the loop body — `last_valid_path = Some(path)` works without the `.clone()`.

nit: The field name `expected_count` is a bit misleading — it's actually the residual counter value (mismatch amount), not a count of expected nodes. The `Display` impl uses it correctly but a reader of the struct definition would expect it to hold the total expected count. Something like `count_difference` or `remaining_count` would be clearer.
